issues.summary: High - Session Fixation

content.text: An attacker who gain's access to an existing session token will be able to gain privileges and assume identity of the affected user.

issues.summary: High - HTTP Host Header Poisoning

content.text: The application trusted the contents of the Host HTTP request header supplied by the client.   Request headers that could be fully controlled by an attacker should be considered untrusted and not used to build any part of the response.  An attacker sending a malicious Host header with a request may be able to perform a cache poisoning attack; depending on the caching options sent in the response this could affect just the browser of an end-user receiving the request, or if a caching proxy is used the attacker may be able to perform the cache poisoning attack against future visitors to the site.  Another vector for Host header poisoning is the password reset request, where an attacker may be able to generate a poisoned password reset link. When clicked by a legitimate user this would pass the reset token to the attacker instead, allowing the attacker to reset the password of the targeted user account.  This vulnerability only affected subdomains of `ahruatpentest.uat.cloudhrmt.oneadvanced.io`. If the host header was changed to any other domain, the request would fail.   However, if changed to any arbitrary and non-existent subdomain, the web server  used the arbitrary host header, allow the user to complete the authentication process and redirect the user to the value provided in the host header.   The server then returned HTTP error 500 to any subsequent request, as it was using the poisoned header value.

issues.comments: Closing card as this has been resolved in attack forge and the fix will be included in next build release with all other vulnerabilities.

issues.summary: High - Unauthenticated API Endpoint Enumeration

content.text: Observed that web portal exposes an unauthenticated, auto-generated API endpoint listing at

. This page discloses the full structure and documentation of the Node Integration Server (NIS) API, including endpoints related to authentication, configuration, file handling, and database connectivity, etc. All of these endpoints (e.g., /config/testJDBC, /upload/sendFile, /login/killSID) are accessible without valid session tokens or authentication headers. This behavior indicates a lack of proper access control enforcement at the API layer. This behavior reflects a broader issue of insecure API design, where endpoint structure is made visible to unauthenticated users, potentially violating the principle of least privilege and API security best practices.

issues.summary: Critical - No Account Lockout May Facilitate Brute Force Password Attack

content.text: Observed that V1DM Smart Suite Web portal don't have account lockout feature, it may facilitate brute force password attack.
Navigate to the V1DM web portal, capture the login traffic in burpsuite and send it to intruder
Go to Intruder ---> select Attack Type---> Sniper ---> Highlight the password and click Add § to mark it as a payload position
Go to the Payloads tab in Intruder ---> Set Payload Type ---> Simple List ---> Select a password file. E.g: Rockyou.txt  ---> Attack
Upon burteforce attack completion enter valid Username and Enter valid Password.
Observe that user is still getting logged, therefore no-account lockout policy is implemented.
